hub_subtitle: Conversação para Viagens, Encontros Reais e Cultura

lesson.menu_title: The Art of Small Talk -- Breaking Ice Without Breaking Character
lesson.menu_desc: Starting and steering a real conversation with direct, indirect and follow-up questions -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 -- The Art of Small Talk
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcelo de Azevedo Souza | Aula 5 -- The Art of Small Talk

listenings.text: Hi Marcelo, it's Nadia, from the layover in Reykjavik. I finally found that little place you told me about upstairs. You were right, it was a real hidden gem. I never would have found it on my own. Funny how one small conversation at a boarding gate can change a whole trip. I kept thinking about what you said, that word of mouth is worth more than any guidebook. Anyway, I made my flight to Oslo, the jet lag is gone, and I wanted to say thank you for breaking the ice. Most people just stare at their phones and wait. Safe travels, and enjoy Iceland. You are going to love it.

Welcome back to the show. Today, the art of small talk. Here is something most people get wrong: they think small talk is about the weather, but it is really about reading the room. The best conversationalists are not the ones who talk the most. They are the ones who ask a good question and then actually listen to the answer. Notice how a simple follow-up, like what made you decide to do that, opens a door that a yes or no question keeps shut. And do not fear the awkward silence. A short pause is not a failure. It gives the other person room to think. So next time you are stuck at an airport, do not reach for your phone. Break the ice. You never know where the conversation will go.
